Title: Am I Home?
Credits: Created by Adam Smith (amsmith@ucsc.edu). Edited by Trinity Willis

Back door.Body: You face a plain, white door. It's unassuming, familiar but unknown. Are there some new scratches in the wood? You haven't been here in years so it's hard to tell.

Leave1.Body: I never got to say goodbye

Laundry Room.Body: A laundry room. Simple, neat, functional. Two litter boxes sit to the left and you wonder where the cats might be. You take off your shoes and set them on the shoe rack in smooth, practiced motions.

Kitchen.Body: It's a large kitchen with an island in the middle. The walls are hand painted a mix of oranges and yellows with sunflowers dancing along near the ceiling. A few strands of pasta stick to the roof above the stove from spaghetti dinners long past.

Office.Body: You walk through a large arch into the office. A white desk against a line of windows holds the remnants of various projects and papers. Bookshelves line the walls filled to the brim with art supplies.

Living Room.Body: A line of bookshelves piled with books and CDs sit behind two plush sofas with swiring threaded patterns.

InteractiveObject.Body: A fat black cat naps in a cat tree by the front door. She looks up when you enter and purrs anticipatedly.

Hallway.Body: Pictures line the walls. A collage of 12 school pictures, some family pictures, a few kid's paintings. You remember sprinting down this hall many times.

Back Door.Body: The back door leads out onto a covered patio. Dry, rocky soil marks an empty path down the side of the house, broken up only by a large lemon tree.

Guest Bathroom.Body: It's a simple bathroom. Clean, white, well maintained.

Guest Bedroom.Body: This was my room once. It's the cat room now. A gray cat looks out the window from atop a wooden table. A cat tree sits next to some spare food and litter.

Trinity's Room.Body: It's a cluttered, lived in space. Some well-loved stuffed animals sit atop a loft bed. The desk underneath is covered in papers and books. Bookshelves are stocked with knick knacks. This was my home.

Mom's Room.Body: Mom preferred the guest bedroom. This room was too big, too open. Her hand painted dresser sits againts the wall. Two walk in closets frame the door to the bathroom.

Bathroom.Body: We shared this bathroom. Two sinks sit on either side of a large bathtub in the corner. The shower is around the corner, metal rims turning white from constant exposure to water.
